summary: DHH revisits his previous negative experience with Sony customer service by highlighting a comment from another frustrated customer. The commenter describes a Cybershot camera that stopped working and an impossibly frustrating support experience. DHH uses this as evidence that Sony has not improved since his own Vaio warranty issues years earlier. He reiterates his blanket warning against buying Sony products.
key_points: Sony's customer service problems are persistent and systemic, not isolated incidents
Expensive products failing without reason compounds the frustration of unreachable support
User-hostile automated phone systems actively drive customers away from getting help
DHH maintains his consumer boycott stance against Sony years after his original complaint
money_quotes: I'll reiterate my warning: Don't buy Sony.
I was amazed at how such an expensive product could just stop functioning with no apparent reason.
What's the point if you can't get through for 5 hours straight.
key_insight: Brand loyalty erodes permanently when companies combine unreliable products with inaccessible customer support.
